'''Nikola Čotrić''' ({{Langx|sr|Никола Чотрић|translit=Nikola Čotrić}}; born 18 November 1998) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er for the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]], and has played for both [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] and [[Serbia national rugby league team|Serbia]] at international level

He previously played for the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] in the NRL and has played for at representative level for [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] in the [[State of Origin series]] as well as one game for the [[Prime Minister's XIII]] in 2018.

==Background==
Cotric was born in [[Cooma]], [[New South Wales]], and is of [[Serbians|Serbian]] descent.<ref>{{cite web|author=James Hooper |url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/the-mail-next-nrl-superstar-youve-never-heard-of-trouble-in-tiger-town/news-story/ef860a2fe0638ea2b0d5685be1a72c4f |title=NRL: Canberra Raiders Nick Cotric, Wests Tigers contracts, Robbie Farah — The Mail |work=Fox Sports |date=2017-02-02 |access-date=2017-03-04}}</ref>

He played his junior rugby league for the Valley Dragons.

==Playing career==
===Early years===
In 2016, Cotric was a member of the Canberra Raiders' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team, playing 21 games primarily at {{rlp|ce}}.<ref>{{cite web|title=C|date=27 December 2012|url=https://nycdatabase.org/players/c/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150616154602/http://nycdatabase.org/players/c/|url-status=usurped|archive-date=16 June 2015|publisher=NYC Database|access-date=4 March 2017}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=Canberra Raiders - 2016|date=20 October 2016|url=https://nycdatabase.org/teams/canberra-raiders/seasons/nyc2016/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171201035603/https://nycdatabase.org/teams/canberra-raiders/seasons/nyc2016/|url-status=usurped|archive-date=1 December 2017|publisher=NYC Database|access-date=29 November 2017}}</ref> In August 2016, Cotric re-signed with the Canberra outfit on a two-year contract until the end of 2018,<ref>{{cite web |author= |url=https://www.zerotackle.com/nick-cotric-re-signs-with-the-raiders-23191/ |title=Nick Cotric re-signs with the Raiders |publisher=Zero Tackle |date=2016-08-05 |access-date=2017-03-04 |archive-date=4 March 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170304193651/https://www.zerotackle.com/nick-cotric-re-signs-with-the-raiders-23191/ |url-status=dead }}</ref> In 2016, Cotric played for the [[Australian Schoolboys rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.ourfootyteam.com/assrl.php |title=ASSRL Schoolboys rugby league news, Australian Secondary School Rugby League, Australian Schoolboys |publisher=Ourfootyteam.com |access-date=2017-03-04}}</ref> On 5 September 2016, Cotric was named at centre in the 2016 NYC Team of the Year.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/2016-holden-cup-team-of-the-year-announced/tabid/10874/newsid/100970/default.aspx|title=2016 Holden Cup Team of the Year announced|website=NRL.com|date=2016-02-28|access-date=2016-09-05}}</ref>

===2017===
Before the season started, Cotric was groomed to fill in the right-wing flank to fill the void made by the departure of [[Edrick Lee]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.canberratimes.com.au/rugby-league/canberra-raiders/nrl-round-one-canberra-raiders-young-gun-nick-cotric-goes-from-family-kitchen-to-nrl-20170225-gul78q.html |title=NRL round one: Canberra Raiders young gun Nick Cotric goes from family kitchen to NRL |access-date=30 January 2018 |archive-date=30 January 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180130211320/http://www.canberratimes.com.au/rugby-league/canberra-raiders/nrl-round-one-canberra-raiders-young-gun-nick-cotric-goes-from-family-kitchen-to-nrl-20170225-gul78q.html |url-status=dead }}</ref> In February 2017, Cotric was named in the Raiders [[2017 NRL Auckland Nines]] squad.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2017/02/04/2017-downer-nrl-auckland-nines-squads/|title=2017 Downer NRL Auckland Nines squads|date=3 February 2017|website=National Rugby League|access-date=22 November 2021}}</ref> In Round 1 of the [[2017 NRL season]], Cotric made his [[NRL]] debut for the [[Canberra Raiders]] against the [[North Queensland Cowboys]], playing on the wing in the 20-16 Golden Point loss at [[1300SMILES Stadium]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.canberratimes.com.au/story/6035937/nick-cotric-to-make-nrl-debut-in-canberra-raiders-fresh-left-edge-pairing-against-north-queensland-cowboys/|title=Nick Cotric to make NRL debut in Canberra Raiders' fresh left-edge pairing against North Queensland Cowboys|first=Caden|last=Helmers|date=28 February 2017|newspaper=[[The Canberra Times]]|access-date=22 November 2021}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/live-nrl-north-queensland-cowboys-v-canberra-raiders/news-story/4c08f6df6a67676a45c768d3977190a8|title=NRL 2017: Cowboys pinch golden point win despite Raiders incredible defence|date=4 March 2017|website=[[Fox Sports]]|access-date=22 November 2021}}</ref> In Round 3 against the [[Wests Tigers]], Cotric scored his first NRL career try in the 46-6 win at [[Canberra Stadium]].<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|url=https://dsf.newscorpaustralia.com/dailytelegraph/subscription/|title=Dailytelegraph.com.au | Subscribe to The Daily Telegraph for exclusive stories|website=Dsfnewscorpaustralia.com|access-date=22 November 2021}}</ref> On 13 September 2017, Cotric was awarded with Canberra's Rookie of the year at the [[Mal Meninga]] Medal Awards night.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.canberratimes.com.au/rugby-league/canberra-raiders/junior-paulo-takes-out-canberra-raiders-mal-meninga-medal-20170911-gyeuje.html |title=Junior Paulo takes out Canberra Raiders' Mal Meninga Medal |access-date=30 January 2018 |archive-date=31 January 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180131024725/http://www.canberratimes.com.au/rugby-league/canberra-raiders/junior-paulo-takes-out-canberra-raiders-mal-meninga-medal-20170911-gyeuje.html |url-status=dead }}</ref> On 27 September 2017, Cotric received the Rookie of the Year award at the 2017 Dally M Awards night.<ref name="auto"/> Even though the Raiders didn’t make the top 8 into the finals, Cotric capped off a great debut season with him playing in all of the Canberra clubs 24 matches and scoring 16 tries in the [[2017 NRL season]]. On 3 November 2017, Cotric extended his contract with the capital club to the end of the 2020 season.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/green-machine-resign-rising-star-winger-nick-cotric-on-threeyear-deal/news-story/cdbe1eb3724537af2786663dba63cdc0|title=Green Machine lockup young gun|first=Nick|last=Campton|newspaper=Daily Telegraph|date=3 November 2017|access-date=22 November 2021}}</ref> On 19 December 2017, Cotric was selected in [[Brad Fittler]]’s emerging [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] squad.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-canberra-raiders-star-nick-cotric-named-in-brad-fittlers-emerging-blues-squad-20171219-h06ys0.html|title=NRL: Canberra Raiders star Nick Cotric named in Brad Fittler's Emerging Blues squad|first=Caden|last=Helmers|date=19 December 2017|newspaper=[[The Sydney Morning Herald]]|access-date=22 November 2021}}</ref>

===2018===
On 28 May 2018, Cotric was named as 18th man for [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] for Game 1 of the [[2018 State of Origin series]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/taste-of-origin-will-only-make-nsw-blues-prospect-nick-cotric-better-20180528-p4zi06.html|title=Taste of Origin will only make NSW Blues prospect Nick Cotric better|first=Caden|last=Helmers|date=28 May 2018|newspaper=[[The Sydney Morning Herald]]|access-date=22 November 2021}}</ref> Cotric would play some matches at fullback towards the end of the season, filling in for the suspended [[Jack Wighton]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/nick-cotric-named-at-fullback-for-raiders-clash-against-cowboys-20180710-p4zqne.html|title=Nick Cotric named at fullback for Raiders clash against Cowboys|first=David|last=Polkinghorne|date=10 July 2018|newspaper=[[The Sydney Morning Herald]]|access-date=22 November 2021}}</ref> Cotric finished the [[2018 NRL season]] with him playing in all of Canberra's 24 matches and scoring 12 tries. On 6 October 2018, Cotric played for the [[Prime Minister’s XIII]] against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]], playing on the wing and scoring a try in the 34-18 win at [[Port Moresby]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/10/06/prime-ministers-xiii-score-hard-earned-win-over-papua-new-guinea-pms-xiii/|title=After pitch invasion, PM's XIII prevail|date=6 October 2018|website=National Rugby League|access-date=22 November 2021}}</ref> At the end of the season, statistics showed that Cotric was on top of tackle break count with 149, pipping the [[Sydney Roosters]] Grand Final winning fullback [[James Tedesco]] by two.{{fact|date=October 2024}}

===2019===
After a good start to the season, Cotric was selected to represent New South Wales in the [[2019 State of Origin series]]. He played in game 1, which Queensland won 18-14, but was ruled out for Game 2 with an ankle injury.{{fact|date=October 2024}}

In Round 17, Cotric was sent off for a dangerous lifting tackle on St George centre [[Tim Lafai]], and suspended for three matches.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/state-of-origin/teams/new-south-wales/blues-rookie-set-to-lose-origin-spot-after-pretty-sad-injury/news-story/3e7da864a8e697d8bfe55fa8d5758b8c/|title=Injury robs Cotric of Origin spot but Raiders flyer set for quick recovery|website=Foxsports.com.au|date=13 June 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/07/14/cotric-sent-off-for-dangerous-throw-on-lafai/|title=Cotric sent off for dangerous throw on Lafai as Raiders down Dragons|website=Nrl.com|date=14 July 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/07/15/cotric-facing-four-week-ban-over-dangerous-throw-charge/|title=JWH not guilty; Cotric to miss three games|website=Nrl.com|date=16 July 2019}}</ref>

Cotric played a total of 22 games for Canberra in the [[2019 NRL season]] as the club reached their first grand final in 25 years.  He played on the wing in the [[2019 NRL Grand Final]] as they were defeated by the [[Sydney Roosters]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-grand-final-player-ratings-roosters-and-raiders-hits-and-misses-20191006-p52y53.html/|title=NRL grand final player ratings: Roosters and Raiders hits and misses|newspaper=[[The Sydney Morning Herald]]}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/49950757|title=Sydney Roosters beat Canberra Raiders to win NRL Grand Final|work=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/grand-final-ricky-stuart-reaction-canberra-raiders-six-again-jack-wighton/ae39b639-d375-4608-89a6-af6285c3d635/|title=How Ricky Stuart reacted to grand final 'six-again' refereeing shocker|website=Wwos.nine.com.au}}</ref>

On 7 October, Cotric was named in the Australian side for the [[2019 Oceania Cup (rugby league)|Oceania Cup]] fixtures. He made his Test debut in Australia’s win over [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] at [[WIN Stadium]], [[Wollongong]], and also played in Australia’s following match, an unexpected loss to [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] at [[Eden Park]] in [[Auckland]].

===2020===
In round 15 of the [[2020 NRL season]], Cotric scored two tries for Canberra in a 36-16 victory over the Gold Coast at [[Cbus Super Stadium]].  This followed on from the prior in round 14 where he scored two tries against Brisbane.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2020-08-22/nrl-south-sydney-thrashes-manly-roosters-raiders-post-wins/12586060/|title=South Sydney beats Manly in 56-16 NRL thrashing, Roosters and Canberra record victories|newspaper=ABC News|date=22 August 2020}}</ref>

On 22 July 2020, the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] announced the signing of Cotric on a three-year deal starting in the 2021 season and rumoured to be worth around $600,000 per season.<ref>{{cite web | url= https://www.bulldogs.com.au/news/2020/07/22/nick-cotric-signs-three-year-deal-with-the-bulldogs/ | title=Nick Cotric signs three year deal with the Bulldogs | website=Bulldogs.com.au| date=22 July 2020 }}</ref>

Cotric played 23 games for Canberra scoring 14 tries, finishing as the club's top try scorer.  In his final game for Canberra which was the preliminary final, he scored two tries in a 30-10 loss against Melbourne.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/ricky-stuart-storms-out-of-canberra-raiders-press-conference-after-one-question/edab3d4d-0eeb-4960-acc0-89d06797f8e0/|title=Canberra Raiders coach Ricky Stuart storms out of press conference after one question|website=Wwos.nine.com.au}}</ref>

===2021===
Cotric made his debut for Canterbury-Bankstown in round 1 of the [[2021 NRL season]] against [[Newcastle Knights|Newcastle]] which ended in a 32-16 defeat.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2021-03-12/brisbane-broncos-lose-to-parramatta-eels-xavier-coates-injured/13242554/|title=Brisbane Broncos' NRL losing streak continues as Parramatta Eels score comeback 24-16 win; Newcastle Knights beat Bulldogs|newspaper=ABC News|date=12 March 2021}}</ref>

On 30 June, it was announced that Cotric would miss the remainder of the [[2021 NRL season]] with a toe injury.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2021-alex-johnston-hamstring-injury-rabbitohs-javid-bowen-acl-cowboys-injuries-return-dates/news-story/916b1e947a0957af21a0454c0de44cd2/|title=Big Bunnies blow as try-scoring machine faces a month on sidelines: Casualty Ward|website=Foxsports.com.au|date=19 July 2021}}</ref>

On 15 December, it was announced he would return to [[Canberra Raiders|Canberra]] on a three-year deal starting in 2022.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.raiders.com.au/news/2021/12/15/nick-cotric-to-return-to-the-raiders/|title = Nick Cotric to return to the Raiders|date = 14 December 2021}}</ref>

===2022===
In round 20 of the [[2022 NRL season]], Cotric scored two tries for Canberra in a 36-24 victory over the [[Gold Coast Titans|Gold Coast]].  In round 21, Cotric was sent to the sin bin for a dangerous high tackle during Canberra's loss to [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]]. Cotric played a total of 21 games for the club in the 2022 NRL season including both of their finals matches against Melbourne and Parramatta.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2022/07/30/raiders-overpower-titans-to-make-it-three-in-a-row/|title=Raiders overpower Titans to make it three in a row|website=www.nrl.com}}</ref>

===2023===
Cotric played a total of 13 matches for Canberra in the [[2023 NRL season]] as the club finished 8th on the table and qualified for the finals. Cotric played in Canberra's golden point extra-time elimination final loss to [[Newcastle Knights|Newcastle]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2023-canberra-raiders-season-review-jack-wighton-ricky-stuart-jarrod-croker-finals-knights-game-jamal-fogarty/news-story/4befaf00c9ae2de911aed34c6d80c77f/|title=Ricky turned losing a club legend into a winning season. Now for his biggest challenge - Brutal Review|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref>

===2024===
In June 2024, it was reported that he had signed for [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]] on a three-year deal. Cotric was limited to only eight appearances with Canberra in the [[2024 NRL season]] where he scored four tries.<ref>{{cite web|website=Catalans Dragons|date=1 Jun 2024|title=Nick Cotric is a Dragon|url=http://www.catalansdragons.com/en/articles-9/285-11845-nick-cotric-is-a-dragon/}}</ref>

===2025===
In round 1 of the 2025 Super League season, Cotric made his club debut for Catalans in their upset loss against [[Hull F.C.]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c20p9z9lvg7o|title=Two tries for Cust as new-look Hull FC beat Catalans|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
